message:
style: replace .expect() on collation registry probes with structured Internal errors + rustfmt nits

Three tiny cleanups that all landed together after the main
collation-aware NoConflict / IdxInsert commits in this batch.

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs

The NoConflict and IdxInsert opcodes each compute an optional
`collated_*_registry` when the collation probe is needed, then
dereference it inside the hot path via
`.as_ref().expect("collated probe requires registry")`.  The
.expect() would abort the whole VDBE engine if the
compute-or-snapshot step ever produced None at a moment when
`uses_collated_probe == true`, which in turn would take down the
entire connection instead of propagating a normal FrankenError.

Replace both sites with a `let Some(collation_registry) = ...
else { return Err(FrankenError::internal(...)); };` block:

  NoConflict:
    "NoConflict: missing collation registry for collated probe"
  IdxInsert:
    "IdxInsert: missing collation registry for collated unique probe"

Same invariant as before (the caller should never hit this
branch because `uses_collated_probe` is only set when the
snapshot succeeds), but the failure mode is a structured
FrankenError::Internal instead of a panic, so the VDBE engine
can unwind cleanly and the caller sees a proper error.

crates/fsqlite-parser/src/parser.rs

Rustfmt-driven rewrap of the three-token-lookahead check in
parse_result_column() for the `schema.table.*` branch added
earlier in this batch.  The original form:

    && (self.peek_nth(2) == &TokenKind::Star
        || (matches!(self.peek_nth(2), TokenKind::Id(_) | TokenKind::QuotedId(_, _))
            && self.peek_nth(3) == &TokenKind::Dot
            && self.peek_nth(4) == &TokenKind::Star))

is equivalent to but cosmetically uglier than:

    && (self.peek_nth(2) == &TokenKind::Star
        || (matches!(
                self.peek_nth(2),
                TokenKind::Id(_) | TokenKind::QuotedId(_, _)
            ) && self.peek_nth(3) == &TokenKind::Dot
            && self.peek_nth(4) == &TokenKind::Star))

Pure rustfmt; the logical structure is unchanged.

crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs

Another rustfmt nit in qualified_name_matches_table_binding:

    (target.schema.is_none()
        && alias.is_some_and(|alias| alias.eq_ignore_ascii_case(&target.name)))

used to span two lines with a hard wrap between `is_none()` and
`&&`; rustfmt wants it on a single line.  Same behavior, one
fewer line.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -40675,8 +40675,7 @@ fn qualified_name_matches_table_binding(
     name: &QualifiedName,
     alias: Option<&str>,
 ) -> bool {
-    (target.schema.is_none()
-        && alias.is_some_and(|alias| alias.eq_ignore_ascii_case(&target.name)))
+    (target.schema.is_none() && alias.is_some_and(|alias| alias.eq_ignore_ascii_case(&target.name)))
         || (target.schema.is_none() && name.name.eq_ignore_ascii_case(&target.name))
         || target
             .schema
